name: Context Wiring

tasks.title: Wire universal.md injection into ContextBuilder.Build
tasks.body:
Update ContextBuilder.Build in internal/pipeline/context_builder.go to always inject universal.md content under a '## Universal Guidance' heading, regardless of whether the task has a Class field set.

Current behavior (lines 111-121): class guidance is only injected when task.Class != "". universal.md is never loaded.

Required behavior per the task-classes spec (Prompt Assembly section):
1. Always resolve and inject prompts/classes/universal.md under '## Universal Guidance'.
2. When task.Class is empty: also inject prompts/classes/coding/default.md under '## Class Guidance'.
3. When task.Class is set and resolves: inject the resolved class prompt under '## Class Guidance'.
4. When task.Class is set but fails to resolve: fall back to coding/default.md under '## Class Guidance'.

The resolution should use ClassRepository for classified tasks and PromptRepository.ResolveRaw for universal.md and coding/default.md (since those aren't keyed by class config).

Reference the ContextBuilder contract spec and the task-classes spec for the exact assembly order.

tasks.title: Update tests for two-layer class guidance
tasks.description: Update tests for two-layer class guidance
tasks.body:
Add and update tests in internal/pipeline/context_builder_test.go to verify the two-layer injection behavior:

1. Test that universal.md content appears in Build output for every task, regardless of Class field.
2. Test that coding/default.md content appears when task.Class is empty.
3. Test that the resolved class prompt appears (not coding/default.md) when task.Class is set and resolves.
4. Test that coding/default.md appears as fallback when task.Class is set but fails to resolve.
5. Test ordering: '## Universal Guidance' section appears before '## Class Guidance' section.

Use the existing test infrastructure (testutil.Environment, scaffolded .wolfcastle directories). Place universal.md and coding/default.md in the base tier of the test environment. For classified-task tests, add a test class entry via ClassRepository.Reload and place a corresponding prompt file.

tasks.title: Clean behavioral guidance from execute.md
tasks.body:
Review internal/project/templates/prompts/stages/execute.md and remove any behavioral guidance that belongs in class prompts rather than the execute stage prompt.

The execute prompt should contain ONLY system mechanics: boundaries, phase structure (claim/study/implement/validate/record/document/commit/signal), terminal markers, decomposition rules, and wolfcastle CLI usage patterns.

It should NOT contain:
- Coding style guidance (that belongs in coding/default.md or language-specific class prompts)
- Testing philosophy (belongs in class prompts)
- Design principles (belongs in universal.md or class prompts)
- Language-specific advice (belongs in language class prompts)

Currently execute.md is 203 lines. Review each section and determine whether it's a system mechanic (keep) or behavioral guidance (remove, since it now lives in universal.md and coding/default.md).

Be conservative: if guidance is about HOW to use wolfcastle commands or WHEN to emit markers, it stays. If it's about HOW to write good code or tests, it goes (it's already in the class prompts).

Verify that universal.md and coding/default.md already cover any guidance you remove. If something would be lost, note it as a gap rather than silently dropping it.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Context Wiring is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify that Build output contains both ## Universal Guidance and ## Class Guidance sections for classified tasks, and universal + coding/default for unclassified tasks
Verify execute.md contains no coding style or testing philosophy guidance (only system mechanics)

breadcrumbs.text: Wired universal.md injection and coding/default.md fallback into ContextBuilder.Build step 4. Added 4 new tests, updated 5 existing tests.
Added 2 tests: universal guidance with classified tasks, and coding/default.md exclusion when class resolves. 29/29 pass.
Cleaned last behavioral guidance from execute.md (removed 'Focus on one concern at a time'). Filed gap for spec cross-reference step that was lost during Validate delegation.

gaps.description: Spec cross-reference step was removed from execute.md Validate section when it was delegated to class guidance. But spec cross-referencing is a wolfcastle-specific workflow mechanic, not a coding principle, so it doesn't belong in coding/default.md either. It should be restored to execute.md's Validate section or added as a separate post-validate step.

task-0001.objective: Wire universal.md and coding/default.md fallback into ContextBuilder.Build
task-0001.what_happened: Rewrote the class guidance section (step 4) of ContextBuilder.Build in context_builder.go. Split it into two sub-steps: 4a injects universal.md under Universal Guidance (silent skip if file missing), 4b resolves the task's class via ClassRepository, falling back to coding/default.md when the class is empty or resolution fails. Updated 5 existing tests to match new behavior and added 4 new tests covering universal guidance injection, coding/default.md fallback, and the graceful-absence cases.
went_well: Clean separation of universal vs class-specific injection keeps the logic readable
All 27 ContextBuilder tests pass, full suite green
improvements: The fallback path uses a bare discard (_ on the ResolveRaw error), which means a permission error on coding/default.md is indistinguishable from the file not existing. Acceptable for now since prompt files are advisory.
action_items: Author the actual universal.md and coding/default.md prompt files (separate task)

task-0002.objective: Add tests verifying two-layer class guidance injection behavior
task-0002.what_happened: Reviewed existing tests against the 5 requirements. Found that task-0001 already covered most scenarios (universal presence, coding/default fallback, ordering, graceful absence). Added two missing tests: one proving universal.md appears even when a task has a resolved class, another proving coding/default.md is excluded when class resolution succeeds. All 29 ContextBuilder tests pass, full suite green.
went_well: Minimal additions needed; prior task was thorough
Both new tests caught real behavioral edges not covered before
improvements: Could add a table-driven test combining all class/universal/default permutations, but the individual tests are readable enough
action_items: None; test coverage for two-layer injection is complete

task-0003.objective: Remove behavioral guidance from execute.md, keeping only system mechanics
task-0003.what_happened: Reviewed all 203 lines of execute.md. The file had already been substantially cleaned by prior work (Validate section delegated to class guidance, 'list every file' and 'signs you should decompose' removed). Found one remaining piece of behavioral guidance: 'Focus on one concern at a time' on line 29, covered by coding/default.md's 'One reason to change' principle. Removed it. Identified one gap: the spec cross-reference step was removed during Validate cleanup but is a wolfcastle system mechanic, not coding guidance, so it needs a home.
went_well: The prior cleanup was thorough; only one line needed removal
Cross-referencing against universal.md and coding/default.md confirmed no silent coverage gaps except the spec cross-reference
improvements: The spec cross-reference gap should be addressed before this branch merges
action_items: Restore spec cross-reference instruction to execute.md Validate section (it is a system mechanic about verifying wolfcastle specs, not a coding principle)
